evals.expected_output: Extracts knowledge from Airtable bases into a per-run cache as linked entity pages with per-batch Opus reviewer gate and mandatory HITL re-anchor on every new base; user reviews and merges into the vault.
expectations: Names Airtable bases as the source
Names linked entity pages as the destination shape
Mentions the per-batch Opus 4.6 reviewer gate at ≥95%
Mentions mandatory HITL re-anchor on every new base
Mentions that pages stage in ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache, not directly in the vault

evals.prompt: How is /airtable-knowledge-extract launched? Can Claude run it from the current session?
evals.expected_output: No — Pattern B pipeline. Claude prints the supaterm tab invocation (env -u CLAUDECODE bash -c '...' inside `sp tab new --script`) and stops in-session. The bun runtime in run.mts owns the per-base loop; running a 30–60 min orchestration in-session burns ambient Opus context-tokens.
expectations: States the skill is Pattern B (refuse to run in-session)
Mentions launching via supaterm tab (sp tab new --script)
Mentions stripping CLAUDECODE from env (env -u CLAUDECODE) so child workers run outside the parent session
Mentions that Claude prints the invocation block and stops in-session, not Bash it

evals.prompt: Where do entity pages and the outcome log land?
evals.expected_output: Entity pages: ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache/<run-id>/bases/<base-id>/out/<entity-type>/<slug>.md (frontmatter + [[wikilinks]]). Outcome log: <vault>/daily/skill-outcomes/airtable-knowledge-extract.log per skill-spec § 11, appended by run.mts CLI main().
expectations: Entity pages path includes ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache/<run-id>/bases/<base-id>/out/<entity-type>
Mentions frontmatter + wikilinks shape on entity pages
Outcome log path is <vault>/daily/skill-outcomes/airtable-knowledge-extract.log
References skill-spec § 11 as the format spec
Notes that run.mts (not a separate skill ceremony) does the append

evals.prompt: What happens when the per-batch reviewer fails?
evals.expected_output: Self-improver appends a (raw, corrected, reason) triple to the per-base examples.jsonl, then retries the batch at half parallelism (max(1, floor(P/2))). Two consecutive fail-after-retry outcomes → SIGTERM workers + osascript notify + exit kind=escalated-batch.
expectations: Mentions the (raw, corrected, reason) triple appended to examples.jsonl
Retry runs at half parallelism
Two consecutive fail-after-retry outcomes escalate the rung
Escalation kills workers (SIGTERM) and notifies the user

evals.prompt: How does /airtable-knowledge-extract decide which Airtable bases to process?
evals.expected_output: scripts/active-bases.mts wraps list-bases.mts and applies the rules from references/base-selection-rules.md: inclusion via the operator allowlist at ~/.brain-os/airtable-premium-bases.txt (plain ID, ~regex on name, !override force-include, # comments), then 4 exclusions — archive / copy / test name regexes, and an inactive probe that drops bases with no record createdTime ≥ <current-year>-01-01. Trace JSON at ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache/<run-id>/active-bases.json captures every raw base + verdict. Stdout (or --ids flag) feeds the per-base loop in SKILL.md ## Run.
expectations: Names scripts/active-bases.mts as the entry point that decides the set
Mentions the allowlist file at ~/.brain-os/airtable-premium-bases.txt
Mentions the !-prefix override that force-includes a base
Mentions the four exclusion rules (archive, copy, test, inactive)
Mentions the activity cutoff of <current-year>-01-01 (with AIRTABLE_ACTIVITY_CUTOFF override)
Mentions the trace JSON at ~/.claude/airtable-extract-cache/<run-id>/active-bases.json
Mentions that re-running the same run-id reads the cached trace and skips API probes

evals.prompt: How does the operator review the AI-drafted rubric questions before the diff is scored?
evals.expected_output: By default scripts/rubric-author.mts runs an interactive readline grill: one question at a time with a sample record from the airtable scan, accepting k (keep), d (drop), free-text rewrite, '+' to jump to add-mode (remaining auto-kept), or batch shorthand like 'k 1-5 d 6,7' (each range needs explicit k/d prefix). After the question loop, the operator is invited to add new questions until an empty line. Pass --editor to fall back to the original $EDITOR-based flow for non-interactive contexts. Prompts go to stderr so the SKILL.md supaterm `> /dev/null` redirect doesn't suppress the UI. Full shortcut table: references/rubric-author-flow.md.
expectations: Names the interactive grill as the default flow (not $EDITOR)
Mentions one-question-at-a-time with a concrete sample record
Lists the per-question shortcuts: k / d / free-text rewrite / + (add-mode jump)
Mentions batch shorthand like 'k 1-5 d 6,7' with explicit per-range prefix
Mentions add-mode at the end (empty line ends)
Mentions --editor as the escape hatch for non-interactive contexts
Notes that prompts write to stderr so the supaterm `> /dev/null` redirect on stdout doesn't hide the UI
